Job Role Description:
The the role of the Bishop, along with fulfilling the responsibilities of a Priest, is to:
Teach - A bishop is the principal teacher in his diocese and has a responsibility to preach the Word of God to his people. He must ensure that those delegated to teach in his name, namely priests, teachers, catechists and others, teach the truth.
Govern - This refers to meeting the needs of the local community (material, social, personal and spiritual) as well as ensuring that church laws are observed. He is ultimately responsible for training and supplying priests for parishes, for the finances of the diocese and for all church property. A bishop has the power to make church laws, be a judge in church matters and to enforce observance of these laws. These laws generally relate to worship, preaching, administration of the sacraments, safeguarding the faith and morals of the faithful and religious instruction.
Sanctify - A bishop is responsible for ensuring that the sacraments are administered and has the special authority to ordain priests and to confirm. It is usually the case, then, for a bishop to ordain the priests who are to serve in his diocese and to travel around the diocese and administer the Sacrament of Confirmation. He must also ensure that Mass is celebrated in the diocese every Sunday and on major feast day
Job Role Skills and Experience:
•	Master of Divinity (M.Div.) or equivalent theological degree
•	Fluency in additional languages, particularly Latin or languages prevalent in the parish community (desirable)
•	Experience in pastoral counseling or social work
•	Strong public speaking and communication skills
•	Ability to manage administrative tasks and church infrastructure
•	Demonstrated leadership abilities
•	Ordination as a Catholic Priest by the appropriate ecclesiastical authority
•	Ordination as a Catholic Bishop by the appropriate ecclesiastical authority
•	Completion of a seminary program or theological education
•	Deep commitment to the Catholic faith and adherence to its teachings
•	Capability to live a celibate lifestyle in accordance with church doctrine
•	Willingness to be relocated if needed by the diocese
•	Strong interpersonal skills and compassion
•	Physical and emotional stamina to meet the demands of pastoral care
•	Proficiency in conducting liturgical services and administering sacraments

Job Role Description: As a Security Guard, you will be responsible for ensuring the safety and security of a specific location. You will monitor and control access to the location, respond to emergencies, and report any incidents to appropriate authorities. You will be required to maintain a high level of professionalism and customer service.
Job Role Skills and Experience:
Observation skills: A good security guard should be observant and able to pay close attention to their surroundings to detect any potential threats or suspicious activity.
	Communication skills: Effective communication is essential in security, as guards need to be able to communicate clearly and quickly with other guards, law enforcement, and the public.
	Physical fitness: Security guards must be physically fit and able to stand, walk, and patrol for extended periods of time.
	Problem-solving skills: Security guards must be able to think quickly and respond effectively in emergency situations.
	Knowledge of security procedures: A good security guard should have a thorough understanding of security procedures, such as how to handle emergencies, check identification, and maintain secure access control.
	Attention to detail: Security guards must pay close attention to detail to detect potential security risks or breaches.
	Customer service skills: Security guards often interact with the public, so good customer service skills are essential.
	Ability to work independently and in a team: Security guards may work alone or in a team, so they need to be able to work independently as well as collaboratively with others.
	Conflict resolution skills: Security guards may need to resolve conflicts, such as disagreements between individuals or groups, so they should have good conflict resolution skills.
	Knowledge of technology: Security guards may need to operate security equipment, such as cameras or access control systems, so they should have a basic understanding of technology.

Identify new material growth opportunities for CCIWA with particular focus on education, skills, and employment activities. Success in this role would see a material improvement ($1m net profit pa over the next two years) to organisational sustainability and reduce CCIWAs dependency on the ASA contract whilst providing additional services which align to CCIWAs purpose.
Oversee the Work Integrated Learning, Educational Development Fund, Industry Training Hubs and any new programs brought on as part of the growth opportunities. Success in this role would see CCIWA achieve and exceed the requirements under the related programs.

Leading Culture  Engagement

Our leaders play a pivotal role in shaping and building a diverse culture that unities our people around CCIWAs purpose. You will create an environment of respect and inclusion where people can be themselves and feel safe to share ideas and experiences.
Instrumental in driving engagement, you will create department specific engagement initiatives aligned to CCIWAs broader engagement plan.
You will create strong relationships across the business to build collaboration, foster curiosity, and resilience to deliver CCIWAs strategic outcomes.
As a contemporary people leader, you will inspire with optimism and energy creating a learning environment where people strive for higher performance.

Key Responsibilities

Proactively seek out new material growth opportunities for CCIWA with particular focus on education, skills, and employment activities. Perform research and or business development activities to move these opportunities forwards. Action the related activities required to obtain the new work including internal business case justification/appropriate approvals, completion of client proposals/presentations/tenders/etc.
Proactively seek out and maintain senior relationships with key internal and external stakeholders which act to either strengthen existing relationships/revenue or to grow and develop new offerings.
Oversee and monitor:

Operational strategies to achieve and exceed contractual requirements and reporting requirements in a timely and effective manner
The growth of existing Portfolio market share and client base
Activity occurring across teams including improve processes and performance.


Provide leadership to the Portfolio of teams. Directly mentor and manage Portfolio leads to enhance their own performance and accountabilities as well as assist them to raise the performance and enhance culture of their respective teams. Oversee performance management and team culture initiatives across Portfolio.
Ensure strong communications across Portfolio on progress, achievements, and areas for improvement to build strong team culture with a key focus on ensuring all locations are engaged (such as Bishop See, Osborne Park, regional team, working from home).
Collaborate with Marketing and Communications team to co-develop strategic marketing, promotional and communications strategies to promote and grow Portfolio services and market share.
Directly manage the Education Services unit that implements projects and initiatives funded through CCIWAs Education Development Fund and ensure the work is appropriately communicated to those members who contribute.
Lead the Industry Training Hubs team and manage the Facilitator role(s) directly. Ensure that all contractual obligations are adhered to and that KPIs are met or exceeded.
Oversee the Work Integrated Learning Program and the delivery of the contractual KPIs.
Conduct appropriate levels of self-directed learning, particularly in relation to education, skills, and employment activities policy issues and provide input to CCIWA Advocacy as required.
Ensure development activities are conducted in relation to being on a Board including completing the required Director Professional Development (DPD) requirements to remain a member of the AICD and continue to sit as a member of the Apprenticeships Australia Pty Ltd Board providing governance and strategic direction to the entity.

Job Role Description: About the PositionThis position provides an assertive outreach model to individuals and families who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless. Provision of information, referral, advocacy support and linking participants to services to enhance their quality of life and status in the community through ensuring access to secure housing options. It is an expectation that the candidate will be enrolled in and complete a Diploma of Community Services within 18 months. The Crisis Intervention & Accommodation and Homestay teams within Homelessness Services are part of the Rural and Remote Department based in Mount Isa. Job Role Description:
Role purpose
The RAP Manager coordinates and supports the design, implementation, and monitoring of the AICD’s Reconciliation Action Plan (RAP). Reflecting the importance of the RAP to AICD’s reconciliation journey, the position sits within People and Culture and reports to the Chief People Officer. The role is responsible for coordinating actions across the RAP program, driving collaboration, good practice and urgency in progress on RAP deliverables, monitoring and reporting of RAP progress. Working closely with the AICD’s RAP Working Group, Executives and First Nations Sector Lead, the position will drive RAP coordination and support engagement with internal and external stakeholders relevant to AICD’s RAP objectives. 

Key responsibilities 
RAP Design, Coordination and Implementation
•	Lead coordination and internal collaboration on AICD’s Innovate RAP deliverables, supporting the RAP Working Group co-Chairs. 
•	Support the RAP Working Group through agendas, papers and progress reports.
•	Work with AICD’s project office on monitoring, reporting and risks across the RAP program, including relevant Executive Committee and Board reports and engagements.
•	Work with AICD’s People & Culture team on monitoring, reporting and risks with AICD’s cultural competency growth and internal change program.
•	Establish professional networks and relationships to support good practice and approaches
•	Role model AICD’s values as a project leader. 

Consultation and Partnerships
•	Lead liaison with Reconciliation Australia to ensure AICD is meeting RAP requirements and applying challenge and good practice approaches to support RAP implementation.
•	Working with the RAP Working Group, identify and support partnerships with like-minded RAP organisations relevant to AICD’s RAP deliverables and objectives.
•	Engage with the First Nations members on the RAP Working Group, AICD’s First Nations staff members and other stakeholders to ensure that RAP actions incorporate a diverse range of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander voices and perspectives. 

Internal Stakeholders and Communications
•	In consultation with relevant internal stakeholders, support the design and implementation of a RAP communications strategy to inform members, staff and volunteers of AICD’s RAP progress, aligned to the cultural competency uplift deliverables of the RAP.
•	Consult with RAP deliverable owners and the RAP Working Group to support effective collaboration across the RAP program, identify and escalate risks and resource constraints and monitor progress.
•	Engage with the RAP Working Group and Change Champions to drive cross-AICD coordination, prioritisation and progress on RAP deliverables.
•	Support the RAP Working Group co-Chairs in engagement with, and activation of, the MD & CEO and AICD Chair as RAP Champions. 
•	Collaborate with AICD’s Sector Lead – First Nations, on relevant sector initiatives and programs.

Reporting and Monitoring
•	Monitor RAP deliverables to ensure timeframes are met, activities are consistent with good practice and informed by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander inclusion and self-determination.
•	Prepare and submit regular reports on RAP program, with support from AICD’s project office.
•	Collaborate with the RAP Working Group to identify and respond to road-blocks, delays or risks.

AICD Contribution
•	Contribute to the development of the department operation plan, suggesting ideas that create solutions for the team, and in turn the organisation. 
•	Undertake or contribute to specific National and State projects or initiatives that support AICD’s strategic plan as required. 
•	Proactively contribute feedback and ideas to AICD for continuous improvement and to achieve OKRs, budgets and strategic goals. 

Other duties in line with competencies and skills may be required on an ad hoc or permanent basis
Job Role Skills and Experience:
Essential knowledge, skills and experience
•	Experience in supporting development or delivery of a RAP, or related program.
•	Experience in supporting or driving cross-organisation projects and/or change programs. 
•	Strong project management and administrative skills.
•	Understanding of reconciliation and RAP frameworks.
•	Well-developed organisation skills including capacity to work in a matrix structure.
•	Highly developed interpersonal skills with ability to build strong collaborative relationships internally and externally.

Desirable 
•	Demonstrated experience in supporting delivery of a national organisation RAP.
•	Experience in supporting or leading engagement with First Nations stakeholders.
•	Tertiary qualifications in relevant field. 
•	Knowledge of AICD’s role as the nation’s leading governance education and membership body.
•	Computer literate including office suite and media/stakeholder systems.

Competencies                                                       
•	Entrepreneurial and Commercial Thinking
•	Presenting and Communicating Information
•	Planning and Organising
•	Adapting and Responding to Change
•	Writing and Reporting 
•	Self-Motivation and Moral Courage

Job Role Description:
The Graphics and Media Design Executive supports the development and execution of creative design solutions that elevate ELMO’s profile and reinforce our brand across a wide range of print and digital marketing channels. 
Working closely with the Marketing team, this role designs and redesigns computer graphics, web and landing page assets, logos, illustrations, advertisements, brochures and other visual communications; analyses and plans design frameworks based on project concepts and specifications; and ensures all outputs meet ELMO’s brand, quality, information security and deadline requirements.

Job Role Description: Purpose As a key member of the Senior Leadership team (SLT) the Territory Operations Manager will be responsible for executing the strategic plan across your regional branch network. You will be responsible for driving sustainable financial performance across the branches, improved customer satisfaction and employee engagement.  Responsibilities  As a member of SLT team will offer leadership into the design, delivery, and activation of requirements to support the execution of the Group’s strategy across your dedicated branches. Reporting to the Head of Operations you are responsible for overseeing your regional branches and to provide leadership and direction to enable managers to maintain motivated and engaged teams focused on achieving individual and team performance goals.   As an experienced leader, you’ll be responsible for working at an enterprise level and translating and clarifying strategic vision to your teams.    Lead a team of Branch Managers (direct reports) and the broader team, responsible for the day-to-day operations of our branches.  Be an inspirational leader, who challenges status quo in a constructive manner and supports our employees in being the best versions of themselves.  Driving a customer focused culture where we aim to ensure our customers are ‘wowed’ each and every time they engage with us.  Develop and maintain constructive and collaborative business relationships with key internal and external stakeholders, including customers, suppliers, and community leaders.  Driving strong financial performance across the consolidated group, ensuring financial accountability at the branch level, through the close monitoring and achievement of specific KPIs, mainly: Market Share, Customer Experience, and Profitability.  Responsible for driving the company to achieve and surpass efficiency, sales, profitability, cash flow, business goals and objectives. Driving a safety-first culture where employees feel empowered to ensure that their safety is paramount.  In collaboration with the Head of Operations, develop the annual budget across your areas of responsibility and regularly review performance to budget.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Qualifications & Experience Experience in the oversight and strategic direction of an innovative, customer focused, commercial and/or research and development focused business or business unit. Proven track record of strong leadership, developing high performing teams and creating a positive culture where people genuinely feel valued and are encouraged to contribute. Well-developed analytical, strategic thinking and problem-solving ability Ability to develop and grow high level relationships with both internal and external stakeholders. Ability to relate at all levels from technicians to farmers, suppliers, industry bodies, and key community leaders.  Strong business acumen with sound financial and reporting skills combined with a commitment to risk management, ensuring all governance parameters are met. Proven background in creating a culture of a strong personal commitment to Safety, Quality, Health & Environment Possess an easy going and inspiring style but with high levels of drive, energy, enthusiasm, and commitment.  5+ years’ experience managing a retail location or in a corporate management role. Familiar with the Ag equipment industry, John Deere, and competitive products Proven managerial ability in the areas of financial management, marketing experience and a solid understanding of sales, parts, and service operations. Bachelor’s degree in Agriculture, Business or equivalent experience required.

Job Role Description:
This position holds Authorisations and Delegations
Purchasing Authority Level: $30,000

Role purpose
To lead, develop and execute the AICD’s ASX Sector Strategy with the vision to increase the impact, reach, engagement and relevance of the AICD’s work with ASX directors and organisations. The ASX Lead will establish and drive systems to ensure that the AICD’s work is informed through authentic and effective engagement with key stakeholders. The role will drive improved harmonisation and coordination of AICD’s existing work across all business units, support the development of partnerships and new programs, manage a formal consultative forum as well as deliver targeted initiatives.   

Key responsibilities 
ASX Sector Strategy
•	Lead development of the AICD’s overarching ASX Sector Strategy, including principles and strategic priorities for AICD engagement and partnerships.
•	Establish and maintain internal processes to promote coordination, engagement and visibility of agreed strategic priorities across the AICD’s departments and divisions.
•	Develop effective internal reporting and planning mechanisms to support strategic priorities. 
•	Represent the AICD in strategy consultation and development. 

Partnership and Engagement
•	Lead collaboration on principles for AICD engagement and partnerships.
•	Cultivate key relationships within the ASX sector, including directors, organisations, governance and education bodies, regulators, peak and representational bodies and government.
•	Engage with the ASX Chairs Forum to seek input and endorsement of the strategy. 
•	Negotiate agreements and support the establishment of long-term and strategic partnerships, both internal and external, to increase the impact of AICD offerings and presence.
•	Collaborate with the AICD Senior Leadership team to develop and implement the AICD strategy.
•	Meaningfully engage with division councils and relevant advisory committees, fostering alignment to drive objectives. 

Representation
•	Represent the AICD at a senior level when attending or participating in forums, events, and engaging with government. 
•	In accordance with the AICD’s media policy, represent the AICD in media as required.
•	Regularly engage with ASX stakeholders to inform and facilitate the development and implementation of the AICD ASX Sector Strategy.
•	As appropriate with other kindred organisations to represent AICD and raise AICD’s profile.
•	Engage with event and program stakeholders, along with program clients, advocating the AICD’s ASX sector agenda. 

Impact and Relevance of AICD Offerings
•	In collaboration with the Engagement and the Education and Policy Leadership departments, develop and maintain systems to align AICD programs, partnerships and delivery with strategic priorities and outcomes (including measurement of impact, pricing considerations and partnership objectives).

AICD Contribution
•	Contribute to the development of the department operation plan, suggesting ideas that create solutions for the team, and in turn the organisation. 
•	Undertake or contribute to specific National and State projects or initiatives that support Australian Institute of Company Directors’ strategic plan as required. 
•	Proactively contribute feedback and ideas to Australian Institute of Company Directors for continuous improvement and to achieve OKRs, budgets and strategic goals. 

Other duties in line with competencies and skills may be required on an ad hoc or permanent basis
Job Role Skills and Experience:
Essential knowledge, skills and experience
•	MBA or Degree qualified in Business or commercial / professional discipline. 10 years’ experience in senior role in or working with listed organisations. 
•	Proven ability to lead the development of impact strategies, linking global and societal trends in corporate governance with stakeholder perspectives and AICD values.
•	Demonstrated effective and authentic engagement with ASX stakeholders on governance issues.
•	Strong ability to inspire and influence others, fostering a shared sense of purpose and direction to achieve organisational objectives.
•	Demonstrated knowledge of international and national policies and issues impacting corporate governance and direct exposure of the ASX Corporate Governance Principles. 
•	Strong communication skills with the ability to present logical and compelling arguments.
•	Ability to prepare and execute high quality plans, briefs, submissions, correspondence and other written material.
•	Highly developed negotiation, facilitation and presentation skills at a leadership audience level. 
•	Capacity to work independently, flexibly and responsively to achieve results.
•	Strong project management skills and ability to manage competing priorities.
•	Proactive in anticipating stakeholder needs, demonstrating flexibility, collaboration, and initiative.

Competencies                                                       
•	Planning and organising
•	Working with people
•	Learning and researching
•	Formulating strategies and concepts
•	Persuading and influencing
•	Coping with pressure and setbacks

Job Role Description:
Role purpose
The role of the Data Governance Lead is to expand the AICD’s use of data as a strategic enabler of organisational goals and capability. This position will be responsible for the establishment of a Data Governance as well as driving the efficient and effective operation of the Data Governance functions.

•	Train and educate stakeholders on data governance principles and foster a culture of accountability across the business to properly managed data assets.
•	Drive projects on data lifecycle management initiatives, including data quality, metadata management and privacy protection.
•	Facilitate engaging workshops and data discovery sessions.
•	Develop and maintain relationships with internal and external stakeholders to ensure adherence with the data governance framework and associated data management processes.
•	Report on key metrics and results of data governance activities.

•	Strong knowledge of data privacy and security regulations.
•	Outstanding interpersonal skills and demonstrated experience negotiating, influencing and management of external and internal stakeholders.
•	Engaging leadership skills and ability to drive change.

Strong analytical skills to troubleshoot and resolve engineering issues effectively.

Job Role Description:
Role purpose
Provide support to business staff around AICD enterprise applications, particularly CRM and Sitecore applications. Your role is to ensure application support is delivered efficiently and that business applications are running at optimal capability to support the organisation.

Key responsibilities 
Application Support
•	Develop expertise and provide application support across AICD Microsoft CRM, and Sitecore.
•	Liaise with AICD staff on application related issues to assist them with day-to-day operations.
•	Ensure requests for support are completed in a timely manner and to the highest standard.
•	Create and maintain user/training documentation.
•	Assist staff with learning system functionality and ensuring staff in AICD’s interstate offices receive the same level of support as staff in national office.
•	Support training by delivering custom training programs for CRM and Sitecore.
•	Ensure applications continue to meet the security, capacity, scalability, resilience, and operational requirements of the organisation.   
•	Design, implement and maintain monitoring solutions for applications including performance monitoring, security, and system event logging.  
•	Define and implement policies to manage the platforms securely, including identity and access management, patching, and disaster recovery.

Service Desk Coordination
•	Monitor IT service levels and ensure delivery of high-quality service to business users.
•	Liaise with business users to understand their needs and adapt IT services to satisfy.
•	Create and maintain a Service Catalogue that is representative and useful as a communication tool between IT and business. 
•	Ensure successful implementation of ITIL core processes including incident management, problem management and change management.
•	Create and maintain monthly IT service reports to provide visibility to management team. 
•	Create and maintain monthly business units reports to be delivered to managers showing technology services delivered to their specific business unit.
•	Proactively coordinate resolution of Incidents and Problems ensuring these are resolved to the satisfaction of end-users.
•	Implement mechanisms to measure end-user satisfaction in relation to digital services.
•	Managing stakeholder engagement and communications. 
•	Liaise with technology team and suppliers to resolve technology-related incidents & problems.
•	Support end-users with their functional & technical queries to assist them with day-to-day business operations.
•	Trouble shooting and hands-on support following system implementations.
•	Identify issues requiring escalation and escalate as appropriate to ensure most efficient use of resources and quickest resolution.
•	Communicate progress of escalated issues to staff and the Technology team to keep them aware of progress and ensure highest possible level of internal customer satisfaction.
•	Provide interstate assistance when required to ensure that AICD’s remote offices receive the same level of support as staff in national office.

IT Support
•	Incidents & Requests: Receives and handles requests for support following agreed procedures. Responds to requests for support by providing information to enable incident resolution and promptly allocates unresolved calls as appropriate. Maintains records and advises relevant persons of actions taken.
•	Problems: Ensures that appropriate action is taken to anticipate, investigate and resolve problems in systems and services. Ensures that such problems are fully documented within the relevant reporting system(s). Coordinates the implementation of agreed remedies and preventative measures. Analyses patterns and trends.
•	Application Support: Drafts and maintains procedures and documentation for applications support. Manages application enhancements to improve business performance. Ensures that all requests for support are dealt with according to set standards and procedures. 

AICD Contribution
•	Contribute to the development of the department operation plan, suggesting ideas that create solutions for the team, and in turn the organisation. 
•	Undertake or contribute to specific National and State projects or initiatives that support AICD’s strategic plan as required. 
•	Proactively contribute feedback and ideas to AICD for continuous improvement and to achieve OKRs, budgets and strategic goals. 

Other duties in line with competencies and skills may be required on an ad hoc or permanent basis

•	Experience in Sitecore environment highly desirable.

Job Role Description: As a Supermarket Manager, your role will involve overseeing the day-to-day operations of a retail store. This will involve managing staff schedules, ensuring that inventory is properly stocked, and implementing pricing strategies to maximize profits. Additionally, you will be responsible for ensuring that safety protocols are followed, and that the store is clean and presentable for customers.
Job Role Skills and Experience:
Retail expertise: A supermarket manager should have extensive knowledge and experience in retail operations, including inventory management, customer service, and merchandising.
	Leadership skills: A supermarket manager should be able to lead and manage a team of employees effectively, providing direction, guidance, and support.
	Communication skills: A supermarket manager should be able to communicate effectively with employees, customers, and suppliers.
	Sales and marketing: A supermarket manager should have a good understanding of sales and marketing principles, including promotions, product placement, and pricing strategies.
	Budgeting and financial management: A supermarket manager should be able to manage budgets, control costs, and analyze financial data.
	Customer service: A supermarket manager should be able to build strong relationships with customers and provide excellent customer service.
	Operational efficiency: A supermarket manager should be able to manage the stores day-to-day operations, including scheduling, ordering, and stocking of inventory.
	Staff development: A supermarket manager should be able to mentor and develop employees, helping them grow professionally.
	Problem-solving skills: A supermarket manager should be able to identify and solve problems quickly and effectively, such as resolving customer complaints or managing staff conflicts.
	Attention to detail: A supermarket manager should have a keen eye for detail and ensure that the store is clean, organized, and well-stocked at all times.

Job Role Title: Family Dispute Resolution Practitioner / Senior Practitioner - Whyalla
Job Role Description: The Family Dispute Resolution Practitioner is responsible for providing a range of high quality, child-focused interventions to families post separation who are experiencing various levels of conflict and need support reaching agreements in relation to their children and/or the distribution of property. This includes intake, assessment, case management, and the provision of interventions including mediation and child inclusive practice under the jurisdiction of the Family Law Act, as well as advocacy and referral services.
Job Role Skills and Experience:
If applying as a qualified practitioner, you will require:


	Graduate Diploma Family Dispute Resolution



	Registration as a Family Dispute Resolution Practitioner with the Attorney-Generals Department


If applying as a trainee, you will require:


	An appropriate degree in, Law, Psychology, Social Work or Social Science



	Be prepared to undertake the Graduate Diploma in Family Dispute Resolution in the first year of employment to achieve registration with the Attorney-Generals Department


All applicants must have:


	An understanding of the Family Law Act



	Sound knowledge of child focused practice as it relates to Family Dispute Resolution (FDR) models



	Sound knowledge of family violence and the impact on children and families



	Knowledge of culturally and linguistically diverse issues as they relate to practice and service delivery



	Knowledge and understanding of Family Relationship Services



	Demonstrated ability to develop, implement and evaluate personal and relationship education



	Demonstrated ability to liaise, negotiate and communicate well, both verbally and in writing



	Demonstrated ability to negotiate and manage conflict



	Ability to undertake Property mediation



	Demonstrated ability to work in a highly confidential manner



	High level organisational and time management skills



	Demonstrated ability to work independently and also in a collaborative manner



	High level of ability in the use of Microsoft Office and Information Technology

Job Role Title: Advisor, Disability - Disability & Community Care Services
Job Role Description: About the PositionCentacare North Queensland’s Client Services Department provides a broad range of services across the North Queensland Region, such as Disabilities, Education and Life Skills, Family and Relationship Counselling, Employee Assistance Services, Gambling Help Services, Family Relationship Centre, Bridge/ROSA Programs, Family Intervention Services and Financial Resilience Counselling.As part of this department, the Disability Services program is an expert team, seeking to assist and positively enhance the lives of others. Our support workers have the privilege of working alongside people every day to ensure they are living a full and active life both in the community and in their home.  Our focus is on achieving goals through developing the skills of people; create positive relationships to enhance community interactions and breakdown the barriers that limit participation and inclusion.This position provides a key liaison role for individuals seeking services through Centacare who have an approved NDIS plan. The role is the first point of contact for potential program participants in Support Coordination to scope both the services required and provide a comprehensive and timely service package for individuals to consider. This role will work closely with NDIS clients to ensure they receive a timely and responsive service offering in line with their support plan.Key Responsibilities IncludeDevelop strong communication and liaison with all current and potential NDIS clients to ensure services offered and implemented are responsive and in line with requirements.Establish, support and design plans to address the identified needs of the participant in line with NDIS plans, individual requirements and NDIA guidelines.Explore, identify, develop, strengthen and facilitate formal and informal networks with individuals and their families to maximise opportunities for participation, independence and inclusion in the broader community.Monitor the support provided to participants to ensure a continued strong linkage between service delivery and the goals, needs and aspirations of participant.Participate in local networking and forums to raise Centacare’s profile as a disability provider of choice.Maintain accurate, timely documentation and reporting; recording of information in appropriate databases and participate in and contribute to ongoing monitoring, evaluation and development of the Program and policies and its activities.Understand and comply with the NDIS Code of Conduct and other such relevant external governing frameworks.Maintain knowledge and understanding of the application of the legislation relevant to the program in state and federal legislation.Contribute to a positive, collaborative and values driven team environment that embraces a culture of ‘best practice’ and innovation. To ensure job flexibility the role may be required to perform any other duties as nominated by Centacare North Queensland consistent with the classification descriptors detailed in the Social, Community, Home Care and Disability Services Industry Award 2010.

Job Role Description:
The Clinical Education Coordinator will provide educational leadership in the development, coordination, delivery and evaluation of clinical and multidisciplinary courses, workshops and seminars for both Family Planning NSW clinical staff and external course participants.

The Clinical Education Coordinator will work directly with the Medical Education Officers and Nurse Educators and liaise with the Family Planning NSW Clinical Services, Education Officer Compliance, Educational Designer, clinicians and subject matter experts to achieve this goal.

The Clinical Education Coordinator may be required to undertake some weekend and after hours work.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead and coordinate the development, delivery and evaluation of high quality reproductive and sexual health education
	Maintain a high standard of education delivery in the programs delivered by Family Planning NSW
	Manage education projects
	Provide direction and mentoring to clinicians, subject matter experts and course administration regarding educational, quality and administrative processes
	Lead the marketing effort to promote Family Planning NSW courses, workshops and seminars
	Lead the implementation of the Continuous Course Improvement Process
	Oversee the management and accreditation of Family Planning NSW courses with RACGP, ACRRM and other accreditation bodies, as applicable



Financial and resource management / administration


	Maintain appropriate resource allocation, and effective management and administrative practices, in accordance with organisational policies, processes and delegations of FPNSW.

Job Role Skills and Experience:
Essential criteria

Essential


	Tertiary qualifications in adult education or equivalent
	Certificate IV in Training and Assessment
	Extensive experience in the development and delivery of competency based education programs
	Experience in online facilitation using Moodle
	Ability to work autonomously with limited supervision
	Ability to successfully manage multiple projects
	High level communication skills (written, oral, liaison, negotiation and presentation)
	Demonstrated ability to work as part of a multidisciplinary team
	Flexible and adaptable approach
	Report writing skills
	Computer literacy


Values

Family Planning NSW is a pro-choice organisation. Staff are expected to fully support an individuals right to choose regarding their pregnancy, whether that be parenting, adoption/foster care or abortion. As an abortion service provider, all FPNSW staff are expected to actively participate in the provision of abortion services within the full scope of the role they are appointed to. For this role, that means that FPNSW Education Service delivers high quality training in pregnancy options, including medical and surgical abortion

You must support the Family Planning NSW values:


	Human rights focus - promoting the rights of all people to reproductive and sexual health
	Integrity - maintaining a strong ethical base, being accountable and transparent
	Inclusiveness - valuing and respecting diversity without judgement
	Equity of access - ensuring access to our services for all including priority populations
	Client centred - placing the needs of the whole person at the centre of our work
	Commitment to excellence - ensuring high standards in all our work
	A just culture  a balanced accountability for both individuals and the organisation


Other requirements


	A Criminal Record Check and/or Working With Children Check is/are required prior to commencement in this role


Desirable criteria


	Knowledge of current reproductive and sexual health issues
	Experience in online course development using Moodle
	Project management skills

Job Role Description:
Role purpose
Provide strategic administrative support to the Chief Information Officer / Chief Operations Officer. Ensure the seamless execution of internal and external initiatives, handle sensitive information with the utmost confidentiality, and facilitate high-level collaboration across teams. Streamline administrative processes to optimise the CIO/COO's efficiency and support the department’s goals. Provide support to the broader Service Delivery, Operations and Information Management department as needed.

Key responsibilities 
Executive Assistance
•	Deliver highly confidential support for the CIO/COO’s office, including sensitive correspondence, contracts, and internal communications.
•	Manage the CIO/COO’s calendar by scheduling meetings, coordinating appointments, and arranging travel plans.
•	Draft responses for approval, manage incoming correspondence, and coordinate proactive communication.
•	Monitor key projects under the CIO/COO's purview, ensuring timelines, objectives, and stakeholder expectations are met.
•	Create reports and presentations, including board papers and executive summaries, ensuring data accuracy and clear messaging.
•	Plan and organise meetings, including securing venues, facilitating agenda creation, recording minutes, and sharing follow-up tasks.
•	Continuously improve and refine systems to enhance administrative effectiveness.
•	Contribute to organisation-wide projects that align with the AICD’s strategic objectives, fostering cross-functional cooperation.

Team Administration
•	Coordinate financial processes for the Service Delivery, Operations and Information Management team, managing expense reports, credit card reconciliations, POs, and invoice submissions.
•	Oversee document control through SharePoint or similar platforms, ensuring proper versioning and access.
•	Compile and analyse reports to provide data-driven insights to the CIO/COO for decision-making purposes.
•	Facilitate team activities to maximize productivity, streamline resourcing, and support training initiatives.
•	Support the budget process by applying controls, ensuring compliance, and leading the annual budget compilation.
•	Provide assistance to the wider department for projects, including scheduling and diary management, reports, or ad-hoc needs.
•	Contribute to organisation-wide projects that align with the AICD’s strategic objectives, fostering cross-functional cooperation.
•	Share feedback and innovative ideas to help refine processes and achieve the team's OKRs.
•	Assist with any other responsibilities consistent with the position's requirements.

AICD Contribution
•	Contribute to the development of the department operation plan, suggesting ideas that create solutions for the team, and in turn the organisation. 
•	Undertake or contribute to specific National and State projects or initiatives that support AICD’s strategic plan as required. 
•	Proactively contribute feedback and ideas to AICD for continuous improvement and to achieve OKRs, budgets and strategic goals. 

Other duties in line with competencies and skills may be required on an ad hoc or permanent basis
Job Role Skills and Experience:
Essential knowledge, skills and experience
•	Prior Executive Assistant experience within a professional environment, ideally supporting senior executives.
•	Tertiary qualifications (preferred).
•	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, databases, and a willingness to learn new technology tools.
•	Exceptional organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities.
•	Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with an ability to build effective relationships.
•	Superior attention to detail, with excellent drafting, proofreading, and editing abilities.

Competencies                                                       
•	Planning and Organising
•	Relating and Networking
•	Writing and Reporting
•	Following Instructions and Procedures
•	Working with People
